methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $130M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Amperon
private.rationale: Same sector (Infrastructure & Logistics), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Earth AI, a Series B infrastructure and logistics company founded by Roman Teslyuk, has raised $20M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a narrow peer universe of two private companies in the sector (AiDash and Amperon); no public comparables exist in this space. A conventional peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage peers meeting minimum sample thresholds.

The stage-progression method forms the primary valuation lever. Using total capital raised as a proxy baseline of $130M, the analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage progression, yielding an implied valuation of $247M. This method is applicable given Earth AI's current stage and comparable financing history within the sector cohort.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $210M (low) to $284M (high), with a median of $247M. The wide spread reflects reliance on a single methodological input and the nascent state of comparable data in infrastructure and logistics technology. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 1.9x multiplier to Earth AI's specific risk and growth profile, and the limited visibility into comparable company trajectories post-Series B in this subsector. Additional revenue traction or customer concentration data would narrow this range materially.
